description: Lock Legal Systems (Common/Civil Law) to an earliest Justinianic civil-law anchor while preserving the later English common-law branch as a scoped subclaim.

checks.reason: The combined node's earliest branch is anchored to the first Codex Justinianus in 529 CE, not the old Medieval era-default placeholder.
Ancient written law codes are contextual legal infrastructure for later civil-law/common-law systems, not a strict direct prerequisite.
Written records and law texts are contextual infrastructure for codified and precedential legal systems.
Philosophy is broad jurisprudential context and should not be treated as a hard production dependency.
